title: LibEIP7702 delegation/implementation lookup, proxy bytecode validation, and admin operations
explanation: Five distinct operations sharing low-level idioms: (1) delegationOf checks the 0xef01 prefix (EIP-7702 delegation magic) at bytecode offset 0, then extracts the 20-byte delegation target at offset 3. The expression `mul(shr(96, mload(0x03)), eq(0xef01, shr(240, mload(0x00))))` does both in one branchless pass — extracts the address candidate AND multiplies by the prefix-match boolean, returning 0 if not a 7702 delegation. (2) isEIP7702Proxy fingerprints the proxy by extcodecopy-ing the runtime bytecode minus CBOR metadata, zeroing the two immutable words at known offsets, and comparing keccak256 against a precomputed hash — robust to any deployment that uses the canonical Solady proxy. (3) upgradeProxyDelegation preserves the upper 96 bits of the impl slot when updating: writes old slot to 0x00, then writes shl(96, newImpl) at 0x0c which overlaps only the lower 20 bytes, then stores back the merged value at 0x00. (4) requestProxyDelegationInitialization uses transient storage (tstore) — the request flag is auto-cleared at end of transaction, perfect for cross-call signaling without persistent storage cost. (5) upgradeProxy uses the right-to-left and() trick: call() evaluates first, then mload(0x00) reads the success indicator the proxy wrote.
when_to_apply: Tooling around EIP-7702 (wallet UIs, indexers, factories) that needs to inspect or manage delegation chains and upgradeable proxies
when_not_to_apply: Pre-Cancun chains (no transient storage), non-Solady proxy implementations where the bytecode hash differs
